arguments.task: Open and read the official Sentry API docs and related first-party pages needed to verify: product description, documented authentication methods, API surface type, rate limit notes, and any first-party mention of MCP/server availability. Then open official Sentry pricing or plan pages to determine self-serve versus gated access tier for building against the API. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with the exact canonical URLs you actually visited and read. Do not rely on search snippets or third-party pages.

input.thought: Start a browser run on official Sentry sources to collect evidence-backed research fields.

raw_result.description: Application monitoring and error-tracking platform with a REST Web API for managing organizations, projects, teams, and data exports.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Sentry API auth docs list Auth Tokens (including personal auth tokens and internal integration tokens), OAuth2 for third-party apps, and DSN/API Keys for event ingestion or legacy use. Mapped as token, oauth2, and other respectively.
raw_result.gating_reason: Sentry pricing lists an API feature across plans, including the free Developer plan, indicating self-serve API access is available without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST Web API (v0) covering account- and project-level resources such as organizations, teams, projects, and data management/export endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence of a Sentry-hosted MCP server for the Sentry API. First-party MCP docs are about using Sentry SDKs to monitor external MCP servers.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs describe fixed-window rate limiting plus concurrent request limits, with limit headers exposed via X-Sentry-Rate-Limit-* response headers.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are comprehensive and canonical, with dedicated pages for API overview, auth, and rate limits; pricing is public and self-serve; API access appears broadly available.

result.description: Application monitoring and error-tracking platform with a REST Web API for managing organizations, projects, teams, and data exports.
result.auth_notes: Official Sentry API auth docs list Auth Tokens (including personal auth tokens and internal integration tokens), OAuth2 for third-party apps, and DSN/API Keys for event ingestion or legacy use. Mapped as token, oauth2, and other respectively.
result.gating_reason: Sentry pricing lists an API feature across plans, including the free Developer plan, indicating self-serve API access is available without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST Web API (v0) covering account- and project-level resources such as organizations, teams, projects, and data management/export endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence of a Sentry-hosted MCP server for the Sentry API. First-party MCP docs are about using Sentry SDKs to monitor external MCP servers.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs describe fixed-window rate limiting plus concurrent request limits, with limit headers exposed via X-Sentry-Rate-Limit-* response headers.
result.notable_signals: Official docs are comprehensive and canonical, with dedicated pages for API overview, auth, and rate limits; pricing is public and self-serve; API access appears broadly available.
